explicit_text: I was drafted into the Vietnam War and served mainly south of Saigon from the late 1960s until spring 1970. I was a platoon leader, company commander, and recon company commander, involved in combat the entire time. The war was a harsh experience, and I was awarded several medals for my service. After returning, I struggled with the lasting effects of Agent Orange but remained proud of serving my country.
